rationale: The EU formally launching the accession process for Ukraine and Moldova is a meaningful geopolitical development for Poland, while Iran peace signals could ease energy market pressure — both are continuations of known trends with modest new momentum.

top_events.title: EU agrees launch of accession process for Ukraine and Moldova
top_events.summary: Formal EU accession launch for Ukraine and Moldova is a significant step for Poland's eastern neighborhood stability and long-term security architecture, though no immediate daily-life change.

top_events.title: Iran signals Hormuz reopening if deal reached
top_events.summary: A potential Iran peace deal could lower global energy prices, offering indirect relief to Polish households still sensitive to gas and oil costs.

top_events.title: China arrests US academic for espionage
top_events.summary: Ongoing US-China friction has limited direct impact on Poland, but contributes to broader geopolitical instability affecting NATO alliance dynamics.
